title: TS: Derive ODEs from reaction systems
description: Implement in src/reactions.ts: (1) deriveODEs(system: ReactionSystem): Model — generate ODE model from reaction stoichiometry and rate laws using standard mass action kinetics. For each reaction with rate k, substrates {Si} with stoichiometries {ni}, products {Pj} with stoichiometries {mj}: rate law v = k * prod(Si^ni), ODE contribution dX/dt += net_stoich_X * v. Handle: source reactions (null substrates — rate is the direct production term), sink reactions (null products — rate is the direct loss term), constraint_equations (appended as additional equations). (2) Generated Model should have: species as state variables, parameters copied, equations as derived ODEs plus constraints, events copied.

title: Julia: Schema validation
description: Implement schema validation in src/validate.jl: (1) validate_schema(data)::Vector{SchemaError} — validate raw JSON against bundled esm-schema.json using JSONSchema.jl. SchemaError struct: path::String, message::String, keyword::String. (2) Integrate into load() — throw on schema violations. (3) Bundle esm-schema.json as package artifact.

title: Author coupling resolution step-by-step algorithm verification tests
Author metadata and provenance test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for ESM metadata section validation: required and optional fields, reference formatting (DOI, URLs), authorship formats, date formatting (ISO 8601), tag validation, license specifications, and metadata schema compliance. Include test cases for missing metadata, invalid dates, malformed DOIs, and proper metadata inheritance in coupled systems. Ensures consistent provenance tracking across all implementations.

title: Julia: Derive ODEs and stoichiometric matrix from reactions
description: Implement reaction system to ODE conversion in src/reactions.jl: (1) derive_odes(rxn_sys::ReactionSystem)::Model — generate ODE model from reaction stoichiometry and rate laws using standard mass action kinetics. Create state variables for each species, parameters for rate constants, equations with d[species]/dt = net_stoichiometry * rate_law. (2) stoichiometric_matrix(rxn_sys::ReactionSystem)::Matrix — compute net stoichiometric matrix (species × reactions, products - substrates). (3) Handle source reactions (null substrates), sink reactions (null products), and constraint equations. Libraries spec Section 4.6 requires Analysis-tier languages implement ODE derivation. Follows libraries spec Section 7.4 mass action algorithm.

title: Editor: CouplingGraph visualization
description: Implement src/components/CouplingGraph.tsx: visual directed graph of the coupling structure. Consumes componentGraph() from esm-format (data-only graph). (1) Layout: use d3-force (or elk.js) for automatic graph layout with physics simulation. (2) Rendering: Solid components for nodes (styled boxes for models, ellipses for data_loaders, diamonds for operators) and edges (SVG paths with arrowheads, different styles per coupling type). (3) Interaction: click a node to highlight it and its connections, click an edge to show coupling detail panel, hover shows tooltip with coupling description. (4) Pan/zoom via CSS transform. (5) Auto-relayout on coupling changes (reactive). (6) Minimap for large graphs.

title: Python: Derive ODEs and stoichiometric matrix
description: Implement src/esm_format/reactions.py: (1) derive_odes(system: ReactionSystem) → Model — mass action kinetics ODE generation from stoichiometry + rate laws. Handle source reactions (null substrates), sink reactions (null products), constraint_equations. (2) stoichiometric_matrix(system: ReactionSystem) → numpy.ndarray — net stoichiometry matrix (species × reactions). (3) substrate_matrix, product_matrix separate.

title: Implement package release and distribution infrastructure
description: Set up automated package publishing for all language implementations: Julia package registration with the General registry, TypeScript package publishing to npm, Python package publishing to PyPI, Rust crate publishing to crates.io, and Go module publishing. Include semantic versioning automation, release notes generation, and cross-library version compatibility tracking. Ensures users can easily install and update the libraries.

title: TypeScript esm-format Code Generation
description: Code generation from ESM files to backend simulation languages. Generates self-contained Julia scripts (using ModelingToolkit/Catalyst syntax) and Python scripts (using esm_format/SymPy). Covers models, reaction systems, variables, equations, reactions, and events. Coupling/domain/solver emitted as TODO comments. Depends on esm-format Core + Analysis.

title: Author comprehensive event testing fixtures
description: Create comprehensive test fixtures covering all event system behaviors: direction-dependent affects, root-finding directions, discrete parameter modification, functional affects, cross-system events, event dependency chains, and reinitialize flag behavior. These fixtures will serve as the foundation for validating all language implementations against the complete event system specification.

title: Go: Parse, validate, pretty-print, substitute
description: Implement in Go: (1) parse.go: Load(path string) (*EsmFile, error) and LoadString(json string) (*EsmFile, error) — parse JSON with encoding/json, validate schema with gojsonschema. (2) validate.go: Validate(file *EsmFile) *ValidationResult — structural validation (equation balance, reference integrity, reaction consistency). (3) display.go: ToUnicode(target interface{}) string and ToLatex(target interface{}) string — pretty-printing with chemical subscripts and operator precedence. (4) substitute.go: Substitute(expr Expr, bindings map[string]Expr) Expr. (5) serialize.go: Save(file *EsmFile) (string, error). (6) Tests using go test with conformance fixtures.

title: Rust: JSON parse, serialize, and schema validation
description: Implement src/lib.rs parse/serialize: (1) pub fn load(json: &str) -> Result<EsmFile, EsmError> — parse JSON with serde_json, validate against bundled schema using jsonschema crate, return typed EsmFile. (2) pub fn save(file: &EsmFile) -> Result<String, EsmError> — serialize to formatted JSON. (3) Handle Expression untagged enum deserialization (serde tries each variant in order: Num, Var, Node). (4) Handle CouplingEntry tagged enum. (5) EsmError enum covering parse errors, schema errors, structural errors. (6) Bundle esm-schema.json via include_str! macro. (7) Round-trip: load(&save(&load(json)?)?).is_ok() for valid files.

title: TS: Python code generation
description: Implement Python code gen in src/codegen.ts: toPythonCode(file: EsmFile): string — generates self-contained Python script. Expression mapping: + → +, * → *, D(x,t) → Derivative(x(t), t), exp → sp.exp, ifelse → sp.Piecewise, Pre → Function('Pre'), ^ → **, grad(x,y) → sp.Derivative(x, y). Output: (1) import statements (sympy, esm_format, scipy). (2) For models: sp.Symbol/sp.Function definitions with comments for units, equation definitions. (3) For reaction systems: species symbols, rate expressions, stoichiometry setup. (4) esm.simulate() call stub with tspan, parameters, initial_conditions. (5) Coupling, domain, solver as TODO comments.

title: Author constraint equation and algebraic variable test fixtures
Python: Expression substitution and structural ops
description: Implement src/esm_format/substitute.py and expression.py: (1) substitute(expr, bindings: dict) → Expr — recursive replacement with scoped reference support. (2) substitute_in_model(model, bindings) → Model. (3) substitute_in_reaction_system(system, bindings) → ReactionSystem. (4) free_variables(expr) → set[str]. (5) contains(expr, var_name) → bool. (6) evaluate(expr, bindings: dict[str, float]) → float — error on unbound variables. (7) simplify(expr) → Expr — constant folding.

title: Implement comprehensive error handling and diagnostics
description: Enhance error handling across all libraries: user-friendly error messages for common mistakes, detailed validation error reporting with fix suggestions, debugging aids for complex coupling issues, performance profiling tools for large models, and interactive error exploration for the editor. Include error code standardization and localization support. Good error handling is essential for user adoption of complex scientific software.

title: TS: JSON parse and serialize
description: Implement src/parse.ts and src/serialize.ts: (1) load(input: string | object): EsmFile — parse JSON string or accept pre-parsed object, validate against schema, throw on malformed JSON or schema violations (with list of SchemaError), return typed EsmFile. (2) save(file: EsmFile): string — serialize to formatted JSON string. (3) Must handle: Expression union type deserialization (number vs string vs ExprNode), CouplingEntry discriminated union, null vs absent fields for optional properties. (4) Round-trip property: load(save(load(json))) === load(json) for all valid files (key ordering/whitespace may differ). Design: parse.ts should separate concerns — JSON parsing, then schema validation, then type coercion.

title: Python esm_format Simulation
description: Simulation tier for Python: ESM-to-SymPy expression conversion and back, SciPy-backed box model simulation via solve_ivp, event handling (continuous events as zero-crossing functions, discrete events via manual stepping), Jupyter integration with rich _repr_latex_ display. Limited to 0D (box model) — spatial operators raise errors. Depends on Python Core.

title: TypeScript esm-format Core Library
description: Pure TypeScript library (zero framework dependencies) providing Core + Analysis tier capabilities: type definitions auto-generated from JSON Schema, JSON parse/serialize, schema validation via ajv, expression pretty-printing (Unicode, LaTeX, ASCII), expression substitution, structural validation, and expression structural operations (free_variables, contains, evaluate). Package name: esm-format. This is the foundation that esm-editor depends on.

title: Author domain and solver configuration test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for domain and solver sections: temporal domain specification, spatial grid definitions, coordinate transforms, boundary/initial condition types, and solver strategy configurations. Include test cases with expected domain discretization and solver setup to validate proper domain interpretation for simulation-capable libraries. These fixtures ensure consistent physical domain representation across implementations.

title: Editor: Selection and inline editing
description: Implement src/primitives/selection.ts and inline editing: (1) Selection state: selectedPath signal tracking the AST path of the currently selected node, isSelected(path) check. (2) Detail panel data: when a node is selected, expose its type, value, parent context, available actions. (3) Inline editing for numbers: double-click a number → input field with current value, Enter confirms, Escape cancels, validates as number. (4) Inline editing for variables: double-click a variable → autocomplete dropdown populated from the current model's declared variables/parameters/species. Filter as user types. Enter or click selects. (5) All changes go through onReplace(path, newExpr) which updates the Solid store at the correct path.

title: TS: Expression pretty-printing
description: Implement src/pretty-print.ts with three formatters: (1) toUnicode(expr: Expr | Equation | Model | ReactionSystem): string — Unicode math notation following spec Section 6.1: chemical subscripts via element-aware tokenizer (O3→O₃), derivative as ∂x/∂t, multiplication as ·, unary minus as − (not hyphen), scientific notation with Unicode superscripts (1.8×10⁻¹²), operator precedence with minimal parentheses. (2) toLatex(expr: ...): string — LaTeX math: fractions as \frac{}{}, derivatives as \frac{\partial}{\partial t}, species in \mathrm{}. (3) toAscii(expr: ...): string — plain text: d(x)/dt, *, e notation. Must include: element lookup table (118 elements), greedy 2-char-before-1-char matching, number formatting rules per spec Section 6.1. Also implement model summary display (spec Section 6.3) for toUnicode(Model) and toUnicode(EsmFile).

title: TS: Project scaffolding and type definitions
description: Initialize the esm-format TypeScript package: (1) Create packages/esm-format/ with package.json (name: esm-format, type: module), tsconfig.json (strict mode, ES2022 target), vitest config. (2) Auto-generate TypeScript types from esm-schema.json using json-schema-to-typescript, then manually augment with: Expr = number | string | ExprNode discriminated union, CouplingEntry discriminated union on 'type' field, DiscreteEventTrigger discriminated union. (3) Create src/types.ts with all type exports: EsmFile, Model, ReactionSystem, Expression/Expr, Equation, AffectEquation, ModelVariable, Species, Reaction, ContinuousEvent, DiscreteEvent, FunctionalAffect, DataLoader, Operator, CouplingEntry (all 6 variants), Domain, Solver, Reference, Metadata. Design: types.ts should be the single source of truth, re-exported from index.ts.

title: Julia: Expression substitution and structural ops
description: Implement src/expression.jl: (1) substitute(expr::Expr, bindings::Dict{String,Expr})::Expr — recursive replacement with scoped reference support. (2) free_variables(expr::Expr)::Set{String}. (3) contains(expr::Expr, var::String)::Bool. (4) evaluate(expr::Expr, bindings::Dict{String,Float64})::Float64 — numerical evaluation, error on unbound variables. (5) simplify(expr::Expr)::Expr — constant folding (2+3→5, x*1→x, x+0→x, x*0→0). All operations are non-mutating (return new Expr objects).

title: Python: Structural validation
description: Implement src/esm_format/validate.py: (1) validate(file: EsmFile) → ValidationResult — schema + structural + unit validation. (2) Structural checks: equation-unknown balance, reference integrity (variable refs, scoped refs, discrete_parameters, coupling refs, operator refs), reaction consistency (species declared, positive stoichiometries, no null-null, rate refs), event consistency (condition types, affect vars, functional affect refs). (3) Return ValidationResult with schema_errors, structural_errors (with codes from spec), unit_warnings, is_valid.

title: Rust: Derive ODEs and stoichiometric matrix from reactions
description: Implement reaction system to ODE conversion in src/reactions.rs: (1) derive_odes(rxn_sys: &ReactionSystem) -> Result<Model, DeriveError> — generate ODE model from reaction stoichiometry and rate laws. Apply standard mass action kinetics: rate law = k * product(substrates^stoichiometry), net_stoich = products - substrates, d[species]/dt = sum(net_stoich * rate_law). (2) stoichiometric_matrix(rxn_sys: &ReactionSystem) -> Vec<Vec<i32>> — compute net stoichiometric matrix. (3) Handle edge cases: source/sink reactions, constraint equations, unit conversions. Return type-safe Result. Libraries spec Section 4.6 requires ALL Analysis-tier languages implement derive_odes. Rust is Analysis tier per Section 5.4.

title: Python: Tests and round-trip verification
description: Implement tests/ with pytest: (1) test_types.py: construct all types programmatically. (2) test_parse.py: load valid fixtures, verify fields. (3) test_roundtrip.py: load(save(load(file))) for all valid fixtures. (4) test_validate_schema.py: verify expected errors for invalid fixtures. (5) test_validate_structural.py: verify error codes. (6) test_display.py: verify Unicode/LaTeX matches display fixtures. (7) test_substitute.py: verify substitution fixtures. (8) test_expression.py: free_variables, contains, evaluate. (9) test_integration.py: full workflow — load, validate, pretty-print, substitute, re-validate.

title: Author substitution test fixtures
description: Create tests/substitution/ fixtures: (1) simple_var_replace.json — array of {input: Expression, bindings: {var: Expression}, expected: Expression} triples: replace variable with number, variable with expression, multiple simultaneous substitutions, nested substitution. (2) nested_substitution.json — substitutions inside nested op nodes (e.g., replace 'T' inside exp(-1370/T)*M). (3) scoped_reference.json — substitution with scoped references: replace 'Model.var' in coupling expressions, replace '_var' placeholder with actual species name.

title: Author scoped reference resolution test fixtures
description: Create comprehensive test fixtures for hierarchical variable references (Section 4.3 of ESM spec): bare references, single-level scoped references (System.variable), multi-level references (A.B.C.variable), subsystem nesting, cross-system references in coupling, and error cases for unresolved references. These fixtures are critical for validating proper variable resolution across all language implementations.

title: Author model editing test fixtures
description: Create tests/editing/ fixtures for model editing operations (libraries spec Section 4): (1) variable_operations.json — test cases for add_variable, remove_variable, rename_variable with {input_model, operation, parameters, expected_model, expected_warnings}. Include cases: adding state/parameter/observed variables, removing unused vs referenced variables (should warn), renaming with reference updates across equations/events/coupling. (2) equation_operations.json — test cases for add_equation, remove_equation, substitute_in_equations. (3) reaction_operations.json — test cases for add_reaction, remove_reaction, add_species, remove_species. (4) coupling_operations.json — test cases for add_coupling, remove_coupling, compose, map_variable operations. (5) merge_extract.json — test cases for merge(file_a, file_b) and extract(file, component_name) operations. Verify editing operations work identically across all Analysis-tier languages.

title: Julia: MTK/Catalyst → ESM conversion
description: Implement reverse conversion: (1) from_mtk_system(sys::ODESystem; name::String)::Model — extract equations, variables, parameters from an ODESystem. Map Differential(t)(var) → D(var,t), symbolic ops → OpExpr. Detect state vs parameter vs observed variable types. Extract events (SymbolicContinuousCallback, SymbolicDiscreteCallback). (2) from_catalyst_system(sys::Catalyst.ReactionSystem; name::String)::ReactionSystem — extract species, parameters, reactions. Map Catalyst Reaction objects to ESM Reaction format.

title: TypeScript esm-format Analysis
description: Analysis-tier features for the TypeScript esm-format library: unit parsing and dimensional analysis, derive_odes from reaction systems, stoichiometric_matrix computation, system graph (component-level) and expression graph (variable-level), graph export to DOT/Mermaid/JSON, and programmatic model editing operations (add/remove/rename variables, equations, reactions, events, coupling). Depends on esm-format Core being complete.

title: TS: Graph export (DOT, Mermaid, JSON)
description: Implement graph serialization in src/graph.ts: (1) toDot(graph: Graph): string — Graphviz DOT format with node shapes (box for models, ellipse for data_loaders, diamond for operators) and edge styles (solid for compose, dashed for variable_map). (2) toMermaid(graph: Graph): string — Mermaid flowchart format for Markdown embedding. (3) toJsonGraph(graph: Graph): string — JSON adjacency list format for web consumption. All three formats for both component-level and expression-level graphs. Node labels should use chemical subscript rendering where appropriate.

title: Create comprehensive documentation and examples
description: Create comprehensive documentation beyond API references: getting started guides for each language, tutorial walking through the complete ESM format, real-world example files demonstrating advanced features, migration guides between format versions, best practices for model authoring, troubleshooting guides for common validation errors, and performance optimization tips. Include the complete minimal example from the spec and several progressively complex examples. Documentation ensures users can effectively use the libraries to build the intended Earth system models.

title: SolidJS esm-editor Interactive Editor
description: SolidJS-based interactive expression and model editor. Renders the AST directly as clickable, editable DOM elements using CSS for math-like layout (no KaTeX/MathJax). Key components: ExpressionNode (core recursive renderer), CSS math layout (fractions, superscripts, subscripts), variable hover highlighting with coupling-aware equivalence classes, inline editing, structural editing, expression palette, ModelEditor, ReactionEditor, CouplingGraph visualization, ValidationPanel, undo/redo, and web component export via solid-element. Package: esm-editor with peer dependency on esm-format.

title: Julia: Coupled system assembly
description: Implement src/coupled.jl: to_coupled_system(file::EsmFile)::EarthSciMLBase.CoupledSystem — full coupling resolution. (1) operator_compose: implement the algorithm from spec Section 4.7.1 — extract dependent variables, apply translations, match equations (direct, translation, _var placeholder expansion), combine matched RHS terms, preserve unmatched. (2) couple2: read connector equations, resolve scoped refs, apply transforms (additive/multiplicative/replacement). (3) variable_map: resolve from/to, apply transform (param_to_var promotes parameter to shared variable, identity, additive, multiplicative, conversion_factor). (4) operator_apply: register Operator in CoupledSystem.ops. (5) Process entries in array order for deterministic naming. Result should be simulatable with OrdinaryDiffEq.jl.

title: Author comprehensive error code classification test fixtures per libraries spec Section 3.4
Author comprehensive graph generation test fixtures
description: Extend existing n2a task to include comprehensive graph generation test fixtures: expression graphs with coupling resolution, graph export format validation, edge relationship types, subsystem hierarchy, cycle detection. These tests will validate graph generation across all output formats and complexity levels including GraphML, DOT, and custom formats.

title: Author event system edge case test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for advanced event system behaviors beyond basic event types: event firing order in complex systems, nested event dependencies, discrete parameter modification chains, event condition evaluation at boundaries (exactly zero, numerical precision issues), affect equation variable scoping, and error conditions (undefined variables in affects, conflicting discrete parameters). These edge cases are critical for robust event system implementation.

title: Editor: ValidationPanel and FileSummary
description: (1) src/components/ValidationPanel.tsx: reactive panel showing validation results (schema errors, structural errors, unit warnings). Updates live via createMemo wrapping validate(). Error items are clickable — clicking an error highlights the offending AST node in the expression editor by setting the selection path from the error's JSON Pointer. Grouped by severity: errors (red), warnings (yellow). Error count badge. (2) src/components/FileSummary.tsx: read-only overview of entire ESM file matching spec Section 6.3 format. Shows: version, metadata, reaction systems summary, models summary, data loaders, coupling rules, domain, solver. Section headers are links that scroll to / select the relevant editor section.

title: TS: Stoichiometric matrix computation
description: Implement in src/reactions.ts: (1) stoichiometricMatrix(system: ReactionSystem): {matrix: number[][], species: string[], reactions: string[]} — compute the net stoichiometric matrix (species × reactions). Convention: rows are species (in declaration order), columns are reactions (in array order). Entry [i][j] = (stoichiometry as product) - (stoichiometry as substrate) for species i in reaction j. Null substrates contribute 0 to substrate stoichiometry; null products contribute 0 to product stoichiometry. (2) substrateMatrix(system): number[][] — substrate stoichiometry only. (3) productMatrix(system): number[][] — product stoichiometry only.

title: Julia: Project scaffolding and type definitions
description: Initialize packages/ESMFormat.jl/ as a Julia package: Project.toml (name=ESMFormat, uuid generated, deps: JSON3, JSONSchema), src/ESMFormat.jl module, test/runtests.jl. Define type hierarchy in src/types.jl: (1) abstract type Expr end with subtypes NumExpr(value::Float64), VarExpr(name::String), OpExpr(op::String, args::Vector{Expr}, wrt::Union{String,Nothing}, dim::Union{String,Nothing}). (2) Equation(lhs::Expr, rhs::Expr), AffectEquation(lhs::String, rhs::Expr). (3) ModelVariable (with type enum: State, Parameter, Observed), Model, Species, Parameter, Reaction, ReactionSystem. (4) ContinuousEvent, DiscreteEvent, FunctionalAffect, DiscreteEventTrigger (abstract with ConditionTrigger, PeriodicTrigger, PresetTimesTrigger). (5) DataLoader, Operator, CouplingEntry (abstract with subtypes for each coupling type). (6) Domain, Solver, Reference, Metadata, EsmFile.

title: TS: Expression substitution
description: Implement src/substitute.ts: (1) substitute(expr: Expr, bindings: Record<string, Expr>): Expr — recursive replacement of variable references with bound expressions. Must handle: variable→constant, variable→expression, _var placeholder→variable name, scoped references (Model.Subsystem.var). (2) substituteInModel(model: Model, bindings: Record<string, Expr>): Model — apply substitution across all equations in a model. (3) substituteInReactionSystem(system: ReactionSystem, bindings: Record<string, Expr>): ReactionSystem — apply across all rate expressions. Design: immutable — returns new objects, does not mutate input. Scoped reference resolution: split on '.', match path through system hierarchy per format spec Section 4.3.

title: Author coupling resolution test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for coupled system resolution: operator composition with placeholder expansion, variable translation with conversion factors, coupling order independence, scoped reference resolution, variable equivalence classes, circular coupling detection. These tests will validate the complex interactions between coupled systems across all implementation languages.

title: Julia: Tests and round-trip verification
description: Implement test/runtests.jl: (1) Type construction tests: create Expr, Model, ReactionSystem programmatically. (2) Parse tests: load each valid test fixture, verify fields. (3) Round-trip tests: load(save(load(file))) == load(file) for all valid fixtures. (4) Schema validation tests: verify expected errors for invalid fixtures. (5) Structural validation tests: verify expected error codes. (6) Pretty-print tests: verify Unicode/LaTeX output matches display fixtures. (7) Substitution tests: verify all substitution fixtures. (8) Expression operation tests: free_variables, contains, evaluate.

title: Go esm-format Core Library (Optional)
description: Optional Go package for server-side tooling, CI/CD validation, and API backends. Minimal scope: parse/serialize with encoding/json, schema validation via gojsonschema, Unicode and LaTeX pretty-printing, structural validation, and substitution. No simulation capability. Lowest priority among all language implementations.

title: Python: System and expression graph generation
description: Implement graph generation functions in src/esm_format/graph.py: (1) component_graph(file: EsmFile) -> Graph[ComponentNode, CouplingEdge] — system-level graph. Return networkx DiGraph or custom graph structure with .nodes(), .edges(), .adjacency() methods. (2) expression_graph(target: Union[EsmFile, Model, ReactionSystem, Equation, Reaction, Expr]) -> Graph[VariableNode, DependencyEdge] — variable-level dependencies. (3) Export functions: to_dot(graph: Graph) -> str, to_mermaid(graph: Graph) -> str, to_json_graph(graph: Graph) -> str for visualization tooling. Include chemical subscript rendering using Unicode characters. Libraries spec Section 4.8 requires ALL Analysis-tier languages implement graph representations for understanding model structure and coupling.

title: Python: SymPy expression conversion
description: Implement src/esm_format/expression.py (SymPy bridge): (1) to_sympy(expr: Expr) → sympy.Expr — convert ESM AST to SymPy expression. Mapping: VarExpr → Symbol or Function(name)(t) for state vars, NumExpr → number, OpExpr('+') → Add, OpExpr('D',wrt='t') → Derivative(f(t),t), OpExpr('exp') → exp, OpExpr('Pre') → Function('Pre'), OpExpr('ifelse') → Piecewise, OpExpr('^') → Pow, OpExpr('grad') → Derivative. (2) from_sympy(sympy_expr) → Expr — reverse conversion for round-tripping through SymPy simplification. (3) symbolic_jacobian(model: Model) → sympy.Matrix — Jacobian of the ODE system.

title: Author simulation test fixtures
description: Create tests/simulation/ fixtures: (1) box_model_ozone.esm — simple 0D O3-NO-NO2 box model (no spatial, no coupling) with known analytical/numerical solution. (2) bouncing_ball.esm — continuous event test (ball bouncing with coefficient of restitution). (3) event_chain.esm — test case with multiple interconnected events including discrete parameter modification and event dependency chains. (4) spatial_limitation.esm — ESM file with spatial operators (grad, laplacian) to verify Python simulation error handling. (5) expected/box_model_ozone.csv — reference trajectory (t, O3, NO, NO2 columns) generated from trusted solver (Julia DifferentialEquations.jl). (6) expected/bouncing_ball.csv — reference trajectory with event times marked. (7) expected/event_chain.csv — reference trajectory for complex event testing. Tolerances: rtol=1e-3 for concentrations.

title: Create ESM format compliance validation matrix
description: Create a comprehensive validation matrix in tests/compliance/ that maps every requirement from esm-spec.md and esm-libraries-spec.md to specific test fixtures. This matrix ensures 100% requirement coverage and serves as the authoritative checklist for conformance testing. Include: (1) compliance_matrix.json - structured mapping from spec requirements to test files (2) requirement_coverage.md - human-readable report of coverage gaps (3) automated tooling to verify matrix completeness (4) CI integration to prevent regressions. This enables systematic verification that all behavioral requirements have corresponding test coverage.

title: Author data loader and operator reference test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for data loaders and operators (ESM spec Sections 8-9): data loader type validation, operator configuration schemas, provides/needed_vars validation, loader_id and operator_id reference checking, temporal/spatial resolution specifications, and interpolation method validation. Include error cases for undefined loader/operator references, configuration schema violations, and variable provision mismatches. These ensure proper runtime component integration.

title: Author security and robustness test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for security and robustness: malformed JSON attack vectors, deeply nested JSON to test parser bombing, very large numbers to test numerical overflow, circular reference detection in expressions and coupling, resource exhaustion scenarios, and input sanitization tests for user-provided expressions. Include tests for safe evaluation of expressions with untrusted input. These ensure libraries are robust against malicious or malformed inputs.

title: Author expression AST round-trip test fixtures
description: Create comprehensive test fixtures for expression AST round-trip fidelity: complex nested expressions with all operators, edge cases for number precision, string variable names with special characters, and expression tree depth limits. Include both valid expressions and expressions that should trigger parsing errors. These tests ensure perfect round-trip preservation: load(save(load(expr))) === load(expr) as specified in libraries spec Section 7.3.

title: Julia: Analysis features
description: Implement analysis features: (1) src/reactions.jl: derive_odes(rs::ReactionSystem)::Model — mass action ODE generation. stoichiometric_matrix(rs::ReactionSystem)::Matrix — net stoichiometry (species × reactions). (2) src/graph.jl: component_graph(file::EsmFile) and expression_graph(file/model/rs/equation/reaction/expr) per spec Section 4.8. Return Graph struct with nodes, edges, adjacency/predecessors/successors methods. Export to DOT, Mermaid, JSON formats. (3) src/units.jl: unit validation using Unitful.jl or DynamicQuantities.jl. (4) src/edit.jl: all editing operations from spec Section 4.

title: Rust: Structural validation
description: Implement src/validate.rs: (1) pub fn validate(file: &EsmFile) -> ValidationResult — schema + structural validation. (2) Structural checks: equation-unknown balance, reference integrity (scoped ref resolution via subsystem hierarchy), reaction consistency, event consistency. (3) ValidationResult: schema_errors, structural_errors (with codes), unit_warnings, is_valid. (4) StructuralError: path, code (enum matching spec codes), message, details.

title: Author expression editor interaction test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures specifically for interactive expression editing: click selection paths, hover highlight equivalence classes, drag-and-drop reordering for commutative operations, structural editing operations (wrap/unwrap/delete), inline editing scenarios (double-click number/variable editing), and undo/redo state transitions. Include test cases for complex nested expressions and cross-system variable highlighting. These fixtures validate the interactive editing behavior requirements from libraries spec Section 5.2.5.

title: Rust: Expression substitution and structural ops
description: Implement src/expression.rs: (1) pub fn substitute(expr: &Expr, bindings: &HashMap<String, Expr>) -> Expr — recursive replacement with scoped reference support. Returns new Expr (immutable). (2) pub fn free_variables(expr: &Expr) -> HashSet<String>. (3) pub fn contains(expr: &Expr, var: &str) -> bool. (4) pub fn evaluate(expr: &Expr, bindings: &HashMap<String, f64>) -> Result<f64, Vec<String>> — numerical evaluation, Err returns unbound variable names. (5) pub fn simplify(expr: &Expr) -> Expr — constant folding. (6) Handle all operators from the schema's enum list.

title: Python: Project scaffolding and type definitions
description: Initialize packages/esm_format/ as a Python package: pyproject.toml (with pytest, jsonschema, sympy, pint deps), src/esm_format/__init__.py. Define types in src/esm_format/types.py using dataclasses: (1) Expr = Union[int, float, str, ExprNode] (ExprNode dataclass with op, args, wrt, dim). (2) Equation(lhs: Expr, rhs: Expr), AffectEquation(lhs: str, rhs: Expr). (3) ModelVariable(type: Literal['state','parameter','observed'], units, default, description, expression), Model, Species, Parameter (for reaction system), Reaction, ReactionSystem. (4) ContinuousEvent, DiscreteEvent, FunctionalAffect, DiscreteEventTrigger variants. (5) DataLoader, Operator, CouplingEntry variants, Domain, Solver, Reference, Metadata, EsmFile. Use Union types and Literal for discriminated unions.

title: Julia: ESM → Catalyst system conversion
description: Implement in src/catalyst.jl: to_catalyst_system(rs::ReactionSystem)::Catalyst.ReactionSystem — convert ESM ReactionSystem to Catalyst.jl ReactionSystem. Map species to @species declarations, parameters to @parameters, reactions to Reaction() objects (handling null substrates for source reactions, null products for sink reactions). Rate expressions converted via the expression mapping. Handle constraint_equations as additional equations. Copy events.

title: Author chemical species name formatting test fixtures
description: Create comprehensive test fixtures for chemical species name formatting (libraries spec Section 6.1): element symbol recognition across all 118 elements, complex chemical formulas (like CH3CHO, Ca(OH)2), edge cases for non-chemical variables (like var2, T, jNO2), multi-part names with underscores, and Unicode/LaTeX formatting differences. Include both positive cases (correct formatting) and negative cases (no formatting change expected) to ensure consistent chemical notation across all libraries.

title: TS: System graph (component-level)
description: Implement in src/graph.ts: componentGraph(file: EsmFile): Graph<ComponentNode, CouplingEdge> — directed graph where nodes are model components and edges are coupling rules. Nodes: one per key in models, reaction_systems, data_loaders, operators, each with {name, type, metadata (var count, eq count, species count)}. Edges from coupling: operator_compose → bidirectional edge, couple2 → bidirectional with coupletype_pair label, variable_map → directed from→to with variable label, operator_apply → directed from operator to modified systems, callback → edge to target, event → directed from condition system to affect system. Graph data structure: {nodes: ComponentNode[], edges: {source, target, data: CouplingEdge}[], adjacency(node), predecessors(node), successors(node)}.

title: Author reaction stoichiometry and mass action test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for reaction system interpretation: mass action kinetics derivation, stoichiometric matrix computation, net stoichiometry calculation, source reactions (null substrates), sink reactions (null products), and complex multi-step reaction networks. Include test cases with expected ODE systems and stoichiometric matrices to validate correct chemical kinetics interpretation across all libraries implementing derive_odes functionality.

title: TS: Expression structural operations
description: Implement in src/expression.ts: (1) freeVariables(expr: Expr): Set<string> — all variable references in the expression tree. (2) freeParameters(expr: Expr, model: Model): Set<string> — subset of freeVariables that are parameters in the given model context. (3) contains(expr: Expr, varName: string): boolean — whether variable appears in expression. (4) evaluate(expr: Expr, bindings: Map<string, number>): number — numerical evaluation; all variables must be bound or throw listing unbound vars. Must handle all operators: arithmetic (+,-,*,/,^), elementary functions (exp, log, sqrt, sin, cos, etc.), comparisons (>,<,==,etc.), logical (and, or, not), ifelse, min, max, floor, ceil, abs, sign. (5) simplify(expr: Expr): Expr — at minimum: constant folding (2+3→5), identity removal (x*1→x, x+0→x), zero multiplication (x*0→0).

title: Editor: Project scaffolding and ExpressionNode core
description: Initialize packages/esm-editor/: package.json (solid-js, solid-element peer deps, esm-format peer dep), vite config with solid plugin, vitest-solid. Implement the core component src/components/ExpressionNode.tsx — recursive AST renderer: (1) Number literal: <span class='esm-num'> with formatted number, click-to-select, hover highlight. (2) Variable reference: <span class='esm-var'> with chemical subscript rendering via renderChemicalName(), hover triggers onHoverVar, highlighted when in highlightedVars set. (3) Operator node: dispatches to OperatorLayout which selects the right layout component based on op type. Props: expr (reactive from Solid store), path ((string|number)[]), highlightedVars (Accessor<Set<string>>), onHoverVar, onSelect, onReplace.

title: Editor: Undo/redo and web component export
description: (1) src/primitives/history.ts: createUndoHistory(file, setFile) → {undo, redo, canUndo, canRedo}. Implementation: capture store snapshots on each setFile call (debounced to avoid capturing every keystroke). Stack size limit (e.g., 100 entries). Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+Z undo, Ctrl+Shift+Z redo. (2) src/primitives/ast-store.ts: Solid store wrapping EsmFile with path-based updates. Central store that all components share. (3) src/web-components.ts: register all editor components as custom elements via solid-element: <esm-expression-editor>, <esm-model-editor>, <esm-file-editor>, <esm-reaction-editor>, <esm-coupling-graph>. Each accepts JSON attributes and emits 'change' CustomEvents. Usable in React, Vue, Svelte, plain HTML.

title: Python: Expression pretty-printing
description: Implement src/esm_format/display.py: (1) to_unicode(target: Expr | Equation | Model | ReactionSystem | EsmFile) → str — Unicode math notation with chemical subscripts (element-aware tokenizer per spec Section 6.1), derivatives as ∂x/∂t, · multiplication, − unary minus, scientific notation with Unicode superscripts. (2) to_latex(target) → str — LaTeX notation. (3) Model summary display per spec Section 6.3. (4) _repr_latex_ methods on EsmFile, Model, ReactionSystem, Equation for Jupyter notebook rich display. (5) ReactionSystem display showing reactions in chemical notation.

title: Rust: Tests and conformance
description: Implement tests/: (1) Round-trip tests for all valid fixtures. (2) Schema validation error tests for invalid fixtures. (3) Structural validation error code tests. (4) Pretty-print tests matching display fixtures. (5) Substitution tests matching fixtures. (6) Expression operation tests. (7) Stoichiometric matrix tests. (8) Graph structure tests matching graph fixtures. Use include_str! to load fixtures. #[test] functions organized by module.

title: TS: Round-trip and conformance tests
description: Implement comprehensive test suite using vitest: (1) Round-trip tests: for every valid .esm fixture, verify load(save(load(file))) produces identical parsed result. (2) Schema validation tests: for every invalid fixture, verify the expected SchemaError codes are returned. (3) Structural validation tests: for every invalid fixture with structural errors, verify expected StructuralError codes. (4) Pretty-print tests: verify Unicode, LaTeX, ASCII output matches display fixtures exactly. (5) Substitution tests: verify all substitution fixtures. (6) Expression operation tests: freeVariables, contains, evaluate with known inputs/outputs. (7) Integration test: load MinimalChemAdvection, validate, pretty-print, substitute T=300, re-validate.

title: Conformance Test Suite
description: Language-independent test fixtures that define the canonical expected behavior for all ESM library implementations. These fixtures are the source of truth for cross-language conformance testing. Must be authored before cross-language testing begins (Phase 1 prerequisite). Includes: valid ESM files, invalid ESM files with expected errors, display format expectations, substitution expectations, graph structure expectations, and simulation trajectory expectations. Stored in tests/ directory alongside the schema.

title: Author end-to-end coupled system simulation test fixtures
description: Create comprehensive end-to-end test fixtures for full coupled system behavior: complete ESM files with all coupling types applied, expected simulation outputs, coupled system assembly verification, cross-system variable flow validation, and operator composition results. Include test cases that exercise the complete workflow from ESM file to simulation results, validating that the entire system produces the expected Earth system model behavior as described in the specifications.

title: Python: SciPy box model simulation
description: Implement src/esm_format/simulate.py: (1) simulate(file: EsmFile, tspan: tuple, parameters: dict, initial_conditions: dict, method: str = 'BDF') → SimulationResult. Steps: resolve coupling (variable_map, operator_compose for 0D only — raise error if spatial operators present), convert expressions to SymPy, generate mass-action ODEs for reaction systems, use sympy.lambdify() for fast NumPy RHS function, optionally generate symbolic Jacobian and lambdify for stiff solvers, call scipy.integrate.solve_ivp(). (2) SimulationResult: t (time points), y (state trajectories), vars (variable names), plot() method using matplotlib. (3) Event handling: continuous events as solve_ivp events parameter (zero-crossing functions), direction-dependent affects via custom direction detection, discrete events via manual stepping.

title: Rust: System and expression graph generation
description: Implement graph generation in src/graph.rs: (1) component_graph(file: &EsmFile) -> Graph<ComponentNode, CouplingEdge> — system-level graph where nodes are model components and edges are coupling rules. Use petgraph::DiGraph or custom structure implementing graph interface. (2) expression_graph(target: &dyn GraphTarget) -> Graph<VariableNode, DependencyEdge> where GraphTarget trait is implemented by EsmFile, Model, ReactionSystem, Equation, Reaction, Expr. (3) Export functions: to_dot, to_mermaid, to_json_graph returning String. Include chemical subscript rendering in labels using Unicode. Libraries spec Section 4.8 requires ALL Analysis-tier languages support graph generation. Rust is Analysis tier per Section 5.4.

title: Author expression evaluation edge case test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for expression evaluation edge cases: unbound variable errors, division by zero, mathematical domain errors, numerical overflow, complex nested expressions, Pre operator evaluation. These tests ensure robust expression engines across all language implementations with comprehensive error handling validation.

title: Author ESM format schema validation edge case fixtures
description: Create edge case test fixtures for JSON schema validation: deeply nested expression trees (test parser recursion limits), very large numbers (scientific notation edge cases), Unicode characters in variable names, empty and null field handling, additional properties validation, and schema evolution test cases. Include both valid edge cases that should pass and invalid cases that should fail with specific error messages. These ensure robust schema validation across all implementations.

title: Editor: Structural editing operations
description: Implement structural editing capabilities: (1) Replace: select a node, type/pick a new expression to replace it entirely. (2) Wrap: select a node, choose an operator from a menu → wraps the node (e.g., select O3, click 'negate' → {op: '-', args: ['O3']}; select expr, click 'exp' → {op: 'exp', args: [expr]}). (3) Unwrap: if selected node is a unary op, replace it with its argument. (4) Delete term: if selected node is an arg of a + or * operation, remove it from the args array (adjusting parent if only one arg remains). (5) Drag-and-drop reordering: for commutative operations (+, *), drag args to reorder. Use HTML5 drag API or pointer events. (6) All operations update through setFile store path-based updates for granular reactivity.

title: Author hierarchical subsystem and scoped reference resolution test fixtures
TS: Unit parsing and dimensional analysis
description: Implement src/units.ts: (1) parseUnit(unitStr: string): DimensionalRep — parse free-form unit strings (mol/mol, cm^3/molec/s, K, m/s, 1/s, s/m, degrees) into canonical dimensional representation. Handle: slash notation, caret exponents, common prefixes. (2) checkDimensions(expr: Expr, unitBindings: Map<string, DimensionalRep>): UnitResult — propagate dimensions through expression tree following rules from spec Section 3.3.1: addition requires same dimensions, multiplication adds dimensions, division subtracts, D(x,t) divides by time dimension, functions require dimensionless args. (3) validateUnits(file: EsmFile): UnitWarning[] — check each equation's LHS vs RHS dimensional consistency. Return warnings (not hard errors) since unit strings may be ambiguous. Recognized tokens: mol, molec, cm, m, km, s, K, J, Pa, ppb, ppm, degrees.

title: Julia ESMFormat.jl Full Tier
description: Full-tier Julia capabilities unique to the EarthSciML ecosystem: bidirectional MTK/Catalyst conversion (to_mtk_system, from_mtk_system, to_catalyst_system, from_catalyst_system), coupled system assembly (to_coupled_system handling operator_compose, couple2, variable_map, operator_apply), derive_odes, stoichiometric_matrix, graph representations, unit validation with Unitful.jl, and model editing operations. Depends on ESMFormat.jl Foundation.

title: Julia: JSON parse and serialize
description: Implement src/parse.jl and src/serialize.jl: (1) ESMFormat.load(path::String)::EsmFile and ESMFormat.load(io::IO)::EsmFile — read JSON, validate schema, construct typed EsmFile. Use JSON3.jl with custom StructTypes for the Expression union type (number→NumExpr, string→VarExpr, object with op→OpExpr), CouplingEntry discriminated union (tag on 'type' field), and DiscreteEventTrigger discriminated union. (2) ESMFormat.save(file::EsmFile, path::String) and ESMFormat.save(file::EsmFile, io::IO) — serialize back to formatted JSON. (3) Handle: null vs missing for optional fields, Expression oneOf deserialization. (4) Round-trip: load(save(load(json))) == load(json).

title: Rust: Project scaffolding and type definitions
description: Initialize packages/esm-format-rs/ as a Rust workspace: Cargo.toml (lib + bin crates), deps: serde, serde_json, jsonschema. Define types in src/types.rs: (1) Expr enum with #[serde(untagged)]: Num(f64), Var(String), Node(ExprNode). ExprNode struct: op: String, args: Vec<Expr>, wrt: Option<String>, dim: Option<String>. (2) Equation, AffectEquation, ModelVariable, Model, Species, Parameter, Reaction, ReactionSystem. (3) CouplingEntry as #[serde(tag='type')] enum: OperatorCompose, Couple2, VariableMap, OperatorApply, Callback, Event. (4) ContinuousEvent, DiscreteEvent, FunctionalAffect, DiscreteEventTrigger enum. (5) DataLoader, Operator, Domain, Solver, Reference, Metadata, EsmFile. All with Serialize/Deserialize derives and proper serde attributes for JSON mapping.

title: Author performance and scalability test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for performance and scalability testing: large reaction networks (hundreds of species and reactions), deeply nested subsystem hierarchies, complex coupling chains with many systems, very large expressions with hundreds of terms, and files at the practical size limits of the format. Include expected performance benchmarks and memory usage baselines. These tests ensure libraries can handle realistic large-scale Earth system models without performance degradation.

title: TS: Structural validation
description: Implement structural validation in src/validate.ts: (1) Equation-unknown balance: for each model, count state variables and D(var,t) equations, report equation_count_mismatch. (2) Reference integrity: every variable in equations exists in model's variables; every scoped reference in coupling resolves (walk subsystem hierarchy per spec Section 4.3); every discrete_parameters entry matches a declared parameter; every from/to in coupling references existing system/variable; every operator_apply references existing operator. (3) Reaction consistency: every species in substrates/products is declared; stoichiometries are positive integers; no null-null reactions; rate expressions only reference declared params/species. (4) Event consistency: continuous event conditions are expressions; discrete condition triggers produce booleans; affect variables are declared; functional affect read_vars/read_params reference declared variables. Return: StructuralError[] with codes from spec Section 3.4.

title: Go: Project scaffolding and type definitions
description: Initialize packages/esm-format-go/ as a Go module: go.mod (module path), go.sum. Define types in types.go: (1) Expr interface with NumExpr, VarExpr, OpExpr implementing it. Custom JSON unmarshal for the Expression union type (try number, string, object). (2) Equation, AffectEquation, ModelVariable, Model, Species, Parameter, Reaction, ReactionSystem structs with json tags. (3) CouplingEntry as interface with concrete types per coupling kind. Custom JSON unmarshal using 'type' field discriminator. (4) Domain, Solver, Reference, Metadata, EsmFile.

title: Author mathematical correctness verification test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for mathematical correctness verification: conservation law validation in reaction systems, mass balance checking, stoichiometric matrix rank analysis, steady-state solution verification, numerical stability testing for stiff systems, and ODE system consistency checking. Include analytical solutions for simple test cases to validate numerical simulation accuracy. These tests ensure the mathematical foundations of the Earth system models are sound.

title: Julia: Structural validation
description: Implement structural validation in src/validate.jl: (1) validate_structural(file::EsmFile)::Vector{StructuralError} with all checks from spec Section 3.2: equation-unknown balance per model, reference integrity (variable refs in equations, scoped refs in coupling via subsystem hierarchy walk, discrete_parameters, coupling from/to, operator_apply refs), reaction consistency (species declared, positive stoichiometries, no null-null, rate refs declared), event consistency (continuous conditions are expressions, discrete conditions produce booleans, affect variables declared, functional affect refs). (2) validate(file::EsmFile)::ValidationResult — combines schema + structural + unit warnings, is_valid field.

title: Author graph test fixtures
description: Create tests/graphs/ fixtures: (1) system_graph.json — for the MinimalChemAdvection file: expected nodes [{name, type, metadata}] and edges [{source, target, coupling_type, label}] at the component level. (2) expression_graph.json — for the SimpleOzone reaction system: expected variable nodes and dependency edges from stoichiometry and rate expressions. (3) coupled_expression_graph.json — full file with coupling resolved: merged variable nodes and cross-system edges via variable_map. (4) expected_dot/ directory with system_graph.dot and expression_graph.dot in Graphviz DOT format. (5) expected_mermaid/ with equivalent Mermaid output.

title: TS: Julia code generation
description: Implement Julia code gen in src/codegen.ts: toJuliaCode(file: EsmFile): string — generates self-contained Julia script. Expression mapping: + → +, * → *, D(x,t) → D(x), exp → exp, ifelse → ifelse, Pre → Pre, ^ → ^, grad(x,y) → Differential(y)(x). Output structure: (1) using statements (ModelingToolkit, Catalyst, EarthSciMLBase, OrdinaryDiffEq). (2) For models: @variables with defaults and units (u"..."), @parameters, equations using ~ syntax. (3) For reaction systems: @species, @parameters, Reaction() array, @named ReactionSystem(). (4) Events mapped to SymbolicContinuousCallback/DiscreteCallback. (5) Coupling, domain, solver as TODO comments with values from file. (6) Parameter defaults with units using Unitful u"..." syntax.

title: Rust: WASM compilation
description: Add WASM target to Rust crate: (1) Cargo.toml: wasm-bindgen dependency, cdylib crate type. (2) src/wasm.rs: #[wasm_bindgen] exports for key functions: load(json: &str) -> JsValue, save(file: JsValue) -> String, validate(json: &str) -> JsValue, to_unicode(json: &str) -> String, to_latex(json: &str) -> String, substitute(json: &str, bindings: &str) -> String. (3) Build with wasm-pack for npm publishing. (4) Package as esm-format-wasm npm package. (5) TypeScript type declarations (.d.ts) for the WASM exports. (6) Benchmark: validate performance comparison TS-native vs WASM for large .esm files.

title: Author invalid ESM test fixtures
description: Create invalid ESM test files in tests/invalid/ with companion tests/invalid/expected_errors.json mapping each file to its expected error codes: (1) missing_esm_version.esm — missing required 'esm' field (schema error), (2) unknown_variable_ref.esm — equation references undeclared variable (structural error: undefined_variable), (3) equation_count_mismatch.esm — more state variables than ODE equations (structural error: equation_count_mismatch), (4) invalid_trigger_type.esm — discrete event with bad trigger type (schema error), (5) circular_coupling.esm — coupling references nonexistent system (structural error: undefined_system), (6) null_reaction.esm — reaction with both substrates and products null (structural error: null_reaction), (7) missing_observed_expr.esm — observed variable without expression field (schema error via if/then). expected_errors.json format: {filename: {error_type: 'schema'|'structural', codes: [...], paths: [...]}}

title: Implement cross-language conformance testing infrastructure
description: Implement CI infrastructure for cross-language conformance testing (libraries spec Section 7.4): (1) Create scripts/test-conformance.sh that runs the same test suite (tests/ directory fixtures) across Julia, TypeScript, Python, and Rust implementations. (2) Ensure each library produces comparable output formats: validation results as JSON, pretty-print outputs as text files, graph exports as DOT/Mermaid/JSON. (3) Add comparison tooling that detects divergence in: schema validation results, structural validation results, display format outputs (unicode, latex), substitution results, graph structure outputs. (4) Configure CI (GitHub Actions) to run cross-language tests on every commit and PR, failing if implementations disagree. (5) Generate conformance report showing which capabilities pass/fail across languages. This ensures libraries maintain fidelity as specified in Section 1.1 goal 5 (interoperability).

title: Author coupling resolution algorithm test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for all coupling resolution algorithms (libraries spec Section 4.7): operator_compose with placeholder expansion, translate mappings, couple2 with additive/multiplicative/replacement transforms, variable_map with all transform types (param_to_var, identity, additive, multiplicative, conversion_factor), and complex multi-step coupling chains. Include edge cases and error conditions to ensure deterministic coupling resolution across all implementations.

title: Julia: Expression pretty-printing
description: Implement src/display.jl: (1) Base.show(io::IO, ::MIME"text/plain", expr::Expr) — Unicode display: chemical subscripts via element-aware tokenizer (matching spec Section 6.1 algorithm exactly), ∂x/∂t derivatives, · for multiplication, − for unary minus, scientific notation with Unicode superscripts. Include static element lookup table (118 symbols). (2) Base.show(io::IO, ::MIME"text/latex", expr::Expr) — LaTeX: \frac{}{}, \partial, \mathrm{} for species. (3) Model display: show(Model) prints equation list, show(EsmFile) prints structured summary per spec Section 6.3. (4) ReactionSystem display: reactions in chemical notation.

title: Julia: System and expression graph generation
description: Implement graph generation functions in src/graph.jl: (1) component_graph(file::EsmFile)::Graph{ComponentNode, CouplingEdge} — system-level graph where nodes are models/reaction_systems/data_loaders/operators and edges are coupling rules. Return SimpleGraph with node metadata. (2) expression_graph(target::Union{EsmFile, Model, ReactionSystem, Equation, Reaction, Expr})::Graph{VariableNode, DependencyEdge} — variable-level dependency graph from equations/reactions. (3) Export functions: to_dot(graph), to_mermaid(graph), to_json(graph) — serialize graphs to DOT/Mermaid/JSON for visualization and interchange. Include chemical subscript rendering in node labels. Libraries spec Section 4.8 requires ALL Analysis-tier languages implement graph representations.

title: Author dimensional analysis and unit system test fixtures
description: Create comprehensive unit validation fixtures beyond basic unit checking: dimensional analysis across equations, unit propagation through complex expressions, conversion factor validation, cross-system unit compatibility in coupling, and scientific unit conventions (mol/mol, molec/cm^3, etc.). Include edge cases for dimensionless quantities, mixed unit systems, and error cases for dimensional inconsistencies. These are critical for validating the scientific correctness of models.

title: Create baseline test directory structure and minimal_chemistry.esm
Author comprehensive expression round-trip and evaluation test fixtures
Verify comprehensive test coverage against specifications
description: Comprehensive verification that all test fixtures properly validate the complete app behavior described in the ESM format and libraries specifications: (1) Verify all 15 sections of the ESM format spec are covered by test fixtures. (2) Verify all capability tiers (Core, Analysis, Interactive, Simulation, Full) have appropriate test coverage. (3) Verify all language-specific libraries (Julia, TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go) have their specified capabilities tested. (4) Verify cross-language conformance testing covers all required behaviors. (5) Create test coverage matrix mapping specifications to test fixtures. This ensures no gaps in validating that implementations produce the app described in the specs.

title: TS: Expression graph (variable-level)
description: Implement in src/graph.ts: (1) expressionGraph(target: EsmFile | Model | ReactionSystem | Equation | Reaction | Expr): Graph<VariableNode, DependencyEdge> — variable-level dependency graph. Nodes: every variable/parameter/species in equations/reactions with {name, kind, units, system}. Edges: for ODE equations, each RHS free variable → LHS variable; for reactions, each substrate/rate-parameter → each affected species. Edge data: {source, target, relationship: 'additive'|'multiplicative'|'rate'|'stoichiometric', equation_index, expression}. (2) File-level: resolve coupling to create cross-system edges (variable_map merges nodes, operator_compose adds operator dependencies). (3) Support merge_coupled option to merge or keep separate equivalent variables.

title: Author valid ESM test fixtures
description: Create the canonical valid ESM test files in tests/valid/: (1) minimal_chemistry.esm — the MinimalChemAdvection example from format spec Section 13 (baseline test for all libraries), (2) full_coupled.esm — exercises ALL sections including models, reaction_systems, data_loaders, operators, coupling (all 6 types), domain (with spatial+temporal+ICs+BCs), and solver, (3) events_all_types.esm — exercises continuous events (with affect_neg, root_find options), discrete events (condition, periodic, preset_times triggers), discrete_parameters, functional affects, and cross-system events, (4) reaction_system_only.esm — valid file with no models section, (5) model_only.esm — valid file with no reaction_systems section. All files must validate against esm-schema.json. Each expected output must be reviewed before commit.

title: Author validation error test fixtures
description: Create tests/invalid/ and tests/validation/ fixtures: (1) Expand tests/invalid/ beyond basic cases to include: missing_required_fields.esm, invalid_enum_values.esm, wrong_data_types.esm, empty_required_arrays.esm. (2) Create expected_errors.json with detailed error expectations with file names mapped to expected_schema_errors and expected_structural_errors arrays. (3) Add structural validation test files: equation_count_mismatch.esm (2 states, 1 ODE), undefined_variable_ref.esm (equation references non-declared variable), circular_coupling.esm (coupling A→B→A), unresolved_scoped_ref.esm (Model.Nonexistent.var), invalid_discrete_param.esm, null_reaction.esm, missing_observed_expr.esm. Each with corresponding error expectations. Reference: libraries spec Section 7.2-7.4.

title: TS: Model editing operations
description: Implement src/edit.ts with immutable editing operations (all return new objects): (1) Variable ops: addVariable(model, name, variable), removeVariable(model, name) with error if referenced, renameVariable(model, oldName, newName) updating equations/events/coupling. (2) Equation ops: addEquation(model, equation), removeEquation(model, indexOrLhs), substituteInEquations(model, bindings). (3) Reaction ops: addReaction(system, reaction), removeReaction(system, id), addSpecies(system, name, species), removeSpecies(system, name). (4) Event ops: addContinuousEvent(model, event), addDiscreteEvent(model, event), removeEvent(model, name). (5) Coupling ops: addCoupling(file, entry), removeCoupling(file, index), compose(file, a, b), mapVariable(file, from, to, transform). (6) File-level: merge(fileA, fileB), extract(file, componentName), deriveODEs (delegate to reactions.ts).

title: Author unit validation test fixtures
description: Create comprehensive unit validation test fixtures for dimensional consistency checking. Since dimensional consistency is critical for model correctness, this task is upgraded to P0 priority. Test fixtures should cover unit conversion, dimensional analysis, unit compatibility checking, and error cases for incompatible units across all mathematical operations and coupling scenarios.

title: Rust esm-format Core Library
description: Rust crate providing Core + Analysis tier: struct definitions with serde derives, JSON parse/serialize, schema validation, expression pretty-printing (Unicode + LaTeX), substitution, structural validation, expression structural operations, unit parsing, derive_odes, stoichiometric_matrix, graph representations, and model editing. Crate: esm-format.

title: Rust: Expression pretty-printing
description: Implement src/display.rs: (1) impl Display for Expr — Unicode output with chemical subscripts (element table as static &[&str; 118]), ∂x/∂t, ·, −, Unicode superscripts for scientific notation. (2) pub fn to_latex(expr: &Expr) -> String — LaTeX output. (3) pub fn to_ascii(expr: &Expr) -> String. (4) impl Display for Model, ReactionSystem, EsmFile — summary display per spec Section 6.3. (5) Operator precedence handling with minimal parentheses. (6) Number formatting: integers plain, 1-4 sig digits decimal, otherwise scientific.

title: Author operator precedence and associativity test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures specifically for expression operator precedence and associativity rules (libraries spec Section 6.1): complex nested expressions with mixed arithmetic operators, function calls, exponentiation, unary operators, and conditional operations. Include edge cases like -(x^2) vs (-x)^2, a+b*c vs (a+b)*c, and deeply nested expressions. These test cases ensure consistent mathematical interpretation across all libraries.

title: Author display format test fixtures
description: Create tests/display/ fixtures: (1) expr_precedence.json — array of {input: Expression, unicode: string, latex: string, ascii: string} triples covering: nested +/* precedence, nested ^, unary minus, division as fraction, D(x,t) derivative, grad(x,y), Pre operator, ifelse conditional, scientific notation numbers (1.8e-12, 2.46e19), n-ary + and * operations, function application (exp, log, sqrt, sin). (2) chemical_subscripts.json — array of {input: string, unicode: string, latex: string} for: O3→O₃, NO2→NO₂, CH2O→CH₂O, H2O2→H₂O₂, k_NO_O3→k_NO_O₃, jNO2→jNO₂, T→T (no change), var2→var2 (no change). (3) model_summary.json — {input: EsmFile (minimal_chemistry.esm content), expected_summary: string} matching the format from libraries spec Section 6.3. (4) number_format.json — {input: number, unicode: string, latex: string, ascii: string} for integers, decimals, scientific notation.
notes: CRITICAL: Must include ASCII format output in addition to Unicode and LaTeX as specified in libraries spec Section 6.1. Current description missing ascii: string field in test fixtures.

title: TS: Schema validation with ajv
description: Implement schema validation in src/validate.ts (schema portion): (1) Bundle esm-schema.json into the package (schema/ directory). (2) Use ajv to compile the schema once at module load. (3) validateSchema(data: unknown): SchemaError[] — returns array of {path: string (JSON Pointer), message: string, keyword: string}. (4) Integrate into load() — schema validation runs automatically on parse and throws if errors found. (5) Handle the schema's anyOf constraint (at least one of models or reaction_systems required). (6) Handle conditional schema (observed variables require expression field).

title: Rust: Analysis features
description: Implement: (1) src/reactions.rs: derive_odes(system) → Model, stoichiometric_matrix(system) → Vec<Vec<i32>> (species × reactions). (2) src/graph.rs: component_graph(file) and expression_graph(file/model/rs/eq/rxn/expr) per spec Section 4.8. Graph struct with nodes, edges, adjacency. Export to DOT, Mermaid, JSON strings. (3) src/units.rs: unit parsing and dimensional checking. (4) src/edit.rs: immutable editing operations. (5) All with comprehensive #[cfg(test)] tests using the conformance test fixtures.

title: Rust: CLI tool
description: Implement CLI binary in src/main.rs (or separate bin/ crate): (1) esm validate <file> — run full validation, print errors/warnings, exit code 0 if valid. (2) esm display <file> [--format=unicode|latex|ascii] — pretty-print file summary. (3) esm extract <file> --component=<name> — extract single model/reaction system as standalone .esm file to stdout. (4) esm diff <file1> <file2> — semantic diff showing added/removed/changed components, equations, reactions (not just text diff). (5) esm stoich <file> --system=<name> — print stoichiometric matrix. (6) esm graph <file> [--level=system|expression] [--system=<name>] [--format=dot|mermaid|json] — graph output to stdout. Use clap for argument parsing. Colored terminal output via termcolor.

title: Editor: CSS math layout components
description: Implement src/layout/ components for mathematical typography using pure CSS (no canvas, no SVG): (1) FractionLayout — display: inline-flex, flex-direction: column for numerator/bar/denominator (for / and D operators). (2) SuperscriptLayout — vertical-align: super, font-size: 0.8em for ^ operator. (3) SubscriptLayout — chemical subscripts and general subscripts. (4) RadicalLayout — CSS-rendered square root symbol for sqrt operator. (5) DelimiterLayout — auto-sizing parentheses that grow with content height. (6) DerivativeLayout — ∂x/∂t as fraction with partial symbols. Base CSS: .esm-frac, .esm-frac-num, .esm-frac-bar, .esm-frac-den, .esm-var, .esm-num, .esm-selected, .esm-hovered, .esm-var-highlighted per spec Section 5.2.4. Use CSS custom properties for theming.

title: Julia: ESM → MTK system conversion
description: Implement src/mtk.jl: to_mtk_system(model::Model)::ODESystem — convert ESM Model to ModelingToolkit ODESystem. Expression mapping: OpExpr('+') → +, OpExpr('D',wrt='t') → Differential(t)(var), OpExpr('exp') → exp, OpExpr('Pre') → Pre, OpExpr('grad',dim='y') → Differential(y)(var), OpExpr('ifelse') → ifelse, NumExpr → literal, VarExpr → @variables/@parameters based on type. Create symbolic variables for state vars as functions of t, parameters as plain symbols. Map equations to MTK ~ syntax. Map continuous events to SymbolicContinuousCallback (with affect_neg, root_find). Map discrete events to SymbolicDiscreteCallback (condition/periodic/preset_times triggers). Handle discrete_parameters and functional_affect (via handler_id registry).

title: Author JSON Schema validation test suite with comprehensive error cases
Editor: Expression palette sidebar
description: Implement src/components/ExpressionPalette.tsx: sidebar with draggable expression templates organized in sections: (1) Calculus: D(_, t), grad(_, x), div(_), laplacian(_). (2) Arithmetic: +, -, *, /, ^, unary minus. (3) Functions: exp, log, sqrt, abs, sin, cos, min, max. (4) Logic: ifelse, >, <, ==, and, or, not. (5) Context-aware: current model's species, parameters, and variables listed for quick insertion. (6) Drag from palette to expression tree to insert at drop position. Drop targets highlighted on drag over. (7) Keyboard shortcut: type '/' in expression to open quick-insert with search filter.

title: Julia ESMFormat.jl Foundation
description: Julia package providing Core tier capabilities for ESM format: type definitions mapping to Julia structs, JSON parse/serialize via JSON3.jl, schema validation via JSONSchema.jl, expression pretty-printing (Unicode + LaTeX), expression substitution, structural validation (equation-unknown balance, reference integrity), and expression structural operations. Julia is the primary language for EarthSciML so this is high priority.

title: Python esm_format Core Library
description: Python package providing Core + Analysis tier capabilities: dataclass type definitions, JSON parse/serialize, schema validation via jsonschema, expression pretty-printing (Unicode + LaTeX), expression substitution, structural validation, expression structural operations, unit validation with pint, derive_odes, stoichiometric_matrix, graph representations, and model editing operations. Package: esm_format.

title: Author comprehensive placeholder expansion (_var) test fixtures
Python: Analysis and Jupyter integration
description: (1) Graph representations in src/esm_format/graph.py: component_graph(file) and expression_graph(file/model/rs/etc.) per spec Section 4.8. Export to DOT, Mermaid, JSON. (2) Unit validation in src/esm_format/units.py using pint. (3) Editing operations in src/esm_format/edit.py. (4) Jupyter integration in src/esm_format/display.py: explore(file) widget showing models, reactions, coupling graph. Enhanced _repr_html_ for rich notebook display.

title: Rust CLI Tool + WASM Target
description: Rust binary crate for CLI tool (esm validate, esm display, esm extract, esm diff, esm stoich, esm graph with DOT/Mermaid/JSON output) and WASM compilation via wasm-bindgen for optional high-performance web use by the TypeScript library. Depends on Rust Core.

title: Editor: ModelEditor and ReactionEditor
description: Implement composed editor components: (1) src/components/ModelEditor.tsx — full model view: variables panel (grouped by type with badges: state/parameter/observed, showing units and defaults), equation list (each as EquationEditor with LHS = RHS), event editors (continuous and discrete), subsystems collapsible. Add/remove variables and equations via UI. (2) src/components/ReactionEditor.tsx — reaction system view: reactions in chemical notation (NO + O₃ →[k] NO₂) with clickable rate expressions that expand to full ExpressionEditor. Species panel, parameter panel. Add/remove reactions via UI. (3) src/components/EquationEditor.tsx — single equation: LHS and RHS as ExpressionEditor instances with = separator.

title: Python: JSON parse, serialize, and schema validation
description: Implement src/esm_format/parse.py and serialize.py: (1) load(path_or_string): EsmFile — load from file path or JSON string, validate against bundled esm-schema.json using jsonschema library, return typed EsmFile. Throw on malformed JSON or schema violations. (2) save(file: EsmFile, path: str = None) → str — serialize to JSON string, optionally write to file. (3) Handle Expression union deserialization (number/string/dict). (4) Bundle esm-schema.json in package data. (5) Round-trip: load(save(load(json))) == load(json).

title: Author ESM format version compatibility test fixtures
description: Create test fixtures for ESM format version handling (libraries spec Section 8): files with different major.minor.patch versions, version compatibility matrix, forward compatibility tests (newer minor versions), backward compatibility tests, version migration tests, and error cases for unsupported versions. Include expected behaviors for each library when encountering different version combinations. Critical for maintaining format evolution compatibility.

title: Author structural validation error test fixtures
description: Create individual test cases for each structural error code defined in libraries spec: equation_count_mismatch, undefined_variable, undefined_species, undefined_parameter, undefined_system, undefined_operator, unresolved_scoped_ref, invalid_discrete_param, null_reaction, missing_observed_expr, event_var_undeclared. Each test case should demonstrate the error condition and expected error response with clear documentation of the triggering conditions.

title: Editor: Variable hover highlighting with equivalence
description: Implement src/primitives/highlighted-var.ts: (1) buildVarEquivalences(file: EsmFile): Map<string, Set<string>> — build equivalence classes from coupling rules using union-find. variable_map entries merge from↔to. operator_compose translate entries merge mapped variables. (2) createHighlightContext() — Solid context providing: [hoveredVar, setHoveredVar] signal, highlightedVars memo (returns Set<string> of all equivalent variables), equivalences memo. (3) Scoping modes: 'equation' (highlight within current equation only), 'model' (within current model, literal name match), 'file' (across all models with equivalence resolution). (4) Scoped reference normalization: both 'O3' and 'SimpleOzone.O3' recognized as same variable in SimpleOzone context. O(1) set lookup for isHighlighted check.
